title: T-061 — Provision + wire planner-solver host (deploy target + env contract + circuit breaker)
prompt:
# T-061 — Planner-solver hosting + env contract

## Goal
PRD §285: the Python solver runs in a separate Python microservice, NOT in Next.js / Vercel. Current tasks only SCAFFOLD it (T-021 code+Dockerfile, T-012 dispatch, T-022/T-023 algorithms); NO task says where it runs → the scheduler cannot run end-to-end. Closes 07-F1. NOTE: contains a HOSTING DECISION (Fly.io/Render/Cloud Run/Railway).

## Scope
1. Decide + document host for services/planner-solver (ADR note): always-on or fast-cold-start container, Postgres connectivity to Supabase pooler (eu-central-2), secret-based auth.
2. Env contract: add PLANNER_SOLVER_URL + PLANNER_SOLVER_SECRET to Vercel env + .env schema; document in Mon-vercel/deploy docs.
3. Next.js side integration: dispatch reads the solver URL/secret, calls the solver, degrades gracefully (circuit breaker → run stays queued/failed with typed error, never 500s).
4. Deploy hook: CI/CD or documented manual step to build+push the solver image (kept out of the Next-only Vercel build).
5. Health probe: wire solver /health into a readiness check.

## Acceptance criteria
- AC1 — A scheduler run dispatched from the deployed Next.js app reaches the deployed solver and returns assignments end-to-end on preview env (live evidence).
- AC2 — Solver-down: run row → typed failed/queued state with user-visible message; no unhandled 500.
- AC3 — Env vars present in Vercel preview; secret never logged (pino redaction).

## Note
Same hosting question applies to the P2 Prophet forecaster (T-054) — fold into the same decision or explicitly defer with P2.
